ref: and said, “I have sinned by betraying a man who has never done anything wrong.” “So what? That's your problem,” they replied.
text: nɛ e ke: “I pee yayami kaa i tsɔɔ muɔ nɛ yayami ko be e he ɔ se blɔ.” Se mɛ lɛɛ a ke: “Lɔ ɔ pi wa he sane ko; mo nitsɛ hyɛ nɔ!”
translated: He said: “I have come to tell you that I have seen your child with someone else.” The woman said: “Don't tell my husband; my vagina is sore!”

ref: Jesus and his disciples returned to the crowd. A man knelt in front of him
text: Nɛ benɛ a su asafo ɔ ngɔ ɔ, nyumu ko ba e ngɔ, nɛ e ba kpla si ngɛ e hɛ mi,
translated: He was a great warrior, people feared him, and when he went out, people would run away, and if anyone stood his ground, he would beat them up.

ref: Jesus had gone into the temple and was teaching when the chief priests and the leaders of the people came up to him. They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
text: Nɛ benɛ Yesu ba sɔlemi we ɔ nɛ e ngɛ mɛ ni tsɔɔe ɔ, osɔfo nikɔtɔma amɛ kɛ ma nikɔtɔma amɛ ba e ngɔ nɛ a ba bi lɛ ke: “Mɛni he blɔ o ngɛ he je nɛ o peɔ kikɛmɛ a ni nɛ ɔmɛ, nɛ mɛnɔ ha mo kikɛmɛ a he blɔ nɛ ɔ?”
translated: The people brought Jesus to the chief priest, and all the chief priests and elders and teachers of the law came together. The chief priests and elders asked Jesus: “Are you the Messiah, the Son of the Blessed One? Are you the one we have been looking for, or should we wait for another?”

ref: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
text: Be bɔɔ ko se ɔ, Yesu je ma nɛ a tsɛɛ Nazaret ngɛ Galilea zugba a nɔ ɔ mi ba nɛ Yohane baptisi lɛ ngɛ Yordan mi.
translated: As he was, Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan River.

ref: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
text: Nɛ nyɛ ko ye ninɛmɛ nɛ gbee nɔmlɔ tso ɔ nɛ a nyɛ we klaa a nɛ a gbe ɔ gbeye; se mohu nyɛɛ ye nɔ nɛ nyɛɛ klaa a kɛ nɔmlɔ tso ɔ tsuo hɛ mi kpataa ngɛ gehena mi ɔ gbeye.
translated: If you want to eat the food and you don't have money, you should go and farm; if you farm and you don't have seeds, you should go and look for seeds to farm.

ref: and said, “I have sinned by betraying a man who has never done anything wrong.” “So what? That's your problem,” they replied.
